page: The Microsoft Azure E64ns_v6 is an Env6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 260 MB of L3 cache, 2 MB of L2 cache per core, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 378.82 GB/sec, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Small-block cached read bandwidth is measured at 4613.81 GB/sec, and large-block write bandwidth is 144.8 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which contribute to a Passmark CPU score of 70602.13. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also strong, with Redis achieving 76.16 million operations per second.

The E64ns_v6 offers high resource density with its 512.0 GB memory capacity, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe storage. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached Premium Storage, which introduces a dependency on network-based I/O. Given the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and strong memory bandwidth, this server is suited for memory-optimized databases, Redis caching layers, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure E64ns_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Env6 family, powered by a 64-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio, but lacks local storage and GPUs. The server demonstrates top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block read speeds reaching 378.82 GB/sec, and strong database performance, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 30184.35 and 76.16 million Redis SET operations per second. Supported by Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets, it delivers strong multi-threaded performance. The absence of local storage is a key tradeoff, requiring network-attached storage. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise-scale web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64ns_v6 is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features top-tier memory bandwidth and is designed for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64ns_v6 is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-bandwidth RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and advanced matrix extensions for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz.
512.0 GB of system memory delivering up to 378.82 GB/sec uncached read bandwidth.
Supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instructions.
No bundled local storage, requiring network-attached Premium Storage.
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, Redis caching, and web serving.
